mnemonics.title: STRIPS Components
mnemonics.content: PAD: Precondition (P), Add (A), Delete (D) - Think 'Precondition Adds and Deletes' to remember the three components

mnemonics.title: Forward vs Backward Search
mnemonics.content: FORWARD: FInd O Replace NEw states - starts From Initial, Forward direction. BACKWARD: Back To Start - starts from Goal, works Backward

mnemonics.title: Planning Graph Purpose
mnemonics.content: SAGE: States And Goals Evolution - captures how states and goals evolve through actions

acronyms.usage: Classic planning representation formalism with Preconditions, Add list, and Delete list
Progressive planning approach starting from initial state
Flexible planning maintaining only necessary ordering constraints
Famous planning system using forward search with helpful heuristics
Converts planning to boolean satisfiability problem

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a tree/graph with circles for states and arrows for actions. Initial state has incoming arrow, goal state has double circle. Path from root to goal represents the plan.
Show alternating levels: State level (fluents true) → Action level (possible actions) → Next State level. Edges show prerequisites and effects connecting levels.
Draw goal at top, show action boxes below. For each action, draw arrow back showing what state preceded it. Work downward like a family tree.
Box with three sections: TOP = Action name, MIDDLE = Preconditions (what must be true), BOTTOM = Split into ADD (left) and DELETE (right) lists.

analogies.analogy: Like navigating a maze where each intersection is a state and each hallway is an action. Finding the exit is like finding a plan.
Like driving from your home (initial state) to a destination (goal) by exploring roads forward, checking if you've arrived at each step.
Like starting at your destination and asking 'How did I get here?' to work backward step by step to find the starting point.
Like packing a suitcase where some items must go in before others but most can be packed in any order - constraints, not a rigid sequence.
Like a storyboard showing scene by scene how the world changes, with each frame (state) and action (transition) mapped out.
Like a recipe: PRECONDITIONS are ingredients you need, ADD LIST is what you add to the dish, DELETE LIST is what gets used up.
